_comment: Model routing table. Each entry maps an agent to its default model and describes why that model was chosen. Override any entry with an environment-specific value — the dispatch script reads this file at invocation time.

speaker.description: Lightweight orchestrator — routes tasks, synthesizes results, communicates with the Spirit
speaker.rationale: Speak-er's job is routing, coordination, and communication. Deep reasoning is not required. Haiku provides fast, low-cost responses appropriate for an orchestrator that handles every message.

checker.description: Mechanical verification — confirms tasks have corresponding implementations
checker.rationale: Check-er does pattern matching and presence/absence verification. It does not need to reason deeply — it needs to be fast and cheap.

tasker.description: Task decomposition and clarity — translates plan features into discrete, measurable tasks
tasker.rationale: Task decomposition requires judgment about ambiguity and measurability. Sonnet provides good reasoning at moderate cost.

planer.description: Structured planning — maintains PLAN.md, ensures feature coherence
planer.rationale: Planning requires multi-step reasoning about dependencies and gaps. Sonnet is appropriate.

prompter.description: Prompt engineering — optimizes prompts for accuracy, concision, and caching
prompter.rationale: Prompter must understand how LLMs process text at a meta level. Sonnet has sufficient capability for this.

deployer.description: DevOps and deployment — scripts, CI/CD, infrastructure operations
deployer.rationale: Deployer handles technical but well-defined work. Sonnet is appropriate.

tester.description: Test authorship — writes unit, integration, and functionality tests
tester.rationale: Good test coverage requires judgment about edge cases. Sonnet provides this at reasonable cost.

reviewer.description: Deep code review — analyzes code quality, security, consistency, and correctness
reviewer.rationale: Code review has high consequence. Subtle security flaws and architectural problems require the best available reasoning. Opus is justified here.

architecter.description: System design — infrastructure decisions, technology choices, communication patterns
architecter.rationale: Architectural decisions are hard to reverse and have long-term consequences. Opus provides the best judgment for high-stakes decisions.

reoriginator.description: Major restructuring — reorganizes the repo for a new major version, may break things intentionally
reoriginator.rationale: Re-Origination-er makes broad, potentially destructive changes. The highest model quality is appropriate when stakes are maximum.

coder.description: Principal-level implementation — writes, modifies, and refactors production-quality code
coder.rationale: Code writing is execution-heavy reasoning over a known spec and existing codebase. Sonnet handles large file contexts well and produces output fast enough that Speak-er is not blocked. Review-er (Opus) is the quality backstop — Code-er does not need to be the final arbiter of correctness.
